issues.title: AD-1 Phase 1: Project foundation and domain contract
issues.body:
## Objective
Create the Electron/React/TypeScript application skeleton and the MVP domain contract.

## Scope
- Application skeleton: Electron main + React renderer + TypeScript (strict)
- Domain contract: `ChatTab`, `AgentDefinition`, `Worker`, `AgentTask`, `PatchSet`, `Conflict`, `MemoryEntry`, `RetrievalQuery`, `McpServerProfile`, `ExtensionManifest`, `IdentitySession`
- Architectural tests (dependency-cruiser)
- Scripts: dev start, typecheck, lint, unit tests

## Definition of Done
- [ ] Project contains directories: `apps/desktop`, `packages/workbench`, `packages/services`, `packages/agent-runtime`, `packages/shared`
- [ ] TypeScript `strict` enabled; configuration separates main/renderer/preload/tests/build
- [ ] Renderer has no direct access to Node APIs except via preload IPC
- [ ] Application launches an empty window with a dark background
- [ ] `docs/domain.md` contains the domain model
- [ ] Architectural tests prevent cycles and imports from `internal`

## Milestone
0.1.x Bootstrap MVP

issues.title: AD-2 Phase 2: Workbench shell and default dark theme
issues.body:
## Objective
Build the primary UI layout inspired by VS Code with a default dark theme.

## Scope
- Layout: activity bar, side bar, editor area, bottom panel, status bar
- Design token system (CSS variables)
- Dark theme baseline meeting WCAG 2.1 AA contrast
- Settings Service for theme persistence

## Definition of Done
- [ ] Initial screen is a functional workbench with an option to open a `.code-workspace`
- [ ] Layout is stable (no dimension shifts on hover/loading/error)
- [ ] UI regions have semantic roles and are keyboard accessible
- [ ] Dark theme is the default and meets WCAG 2.1 AA contrast requirements
- [ ] No flash of light theme on startup

## Milestone
0.1.x Bootstrap MVP

issues.title: AD-3 Phase 3: Workspace Service, Explorer and Search
issues.body:
## Objective
Implement a workspace service with `.code-workspace` parsing, a file tree explorer, and search functionality.

## Scope
- `.code-workspace` parser (JSONC, relative paths, multi-root)
- Explorer: tree view, refresh, reveal in explorer, context menu
- Search: text search, glob include/exclude, results list
- Mark sensitive paths for the Permission Broker

## Definition of Done
- [ ] Workspace Service parses `.code-workspace` with `folders`, `settings`, `extensions`
- [ ] Explorer displays multi-root and reacts to filesystem changes
- [ ] Search returns results with filename, line, and snippet
- [ ] Sensitive paths are flagged and excluded from the model context

## Milestone
0.1.x Bootstrap MVP

issues.title: AD-4 Phase 4: Editor Service with Monaco
issues.body:
## Objective
Integrate Monaco Editor as the text editor with tabs, dirty state, and basic language features.

## Scope
- Monaco: tabs, dirty state, save, reload conflict, split view
- Syntax highlighting for TS/JS/JSON/YAML/Markdown/PowerShell
- Integration with Problems/Diagnostics
- Editor API: open, reveal, applyWorkspaceEdit, showDiff

## Definition of Done
- [ ] Opening a file creates an editor tab
- [ ] Dirty state and save are handled via the Workspace Service
- [ ] External file changes produce reload conflicts
- [ ] Split view supports at least 2 columns
- [ ] Problems panel navigates to Monaco

## Milestone
0.1.x Bootstrap MVP

issues.title: AD-5 Phase 5: Identity Service and GitHub login
issues.body:
## Objective
Implement GitHub login via OAuth (system browser + loopback callback) with secure token storage.

## Scope
- GitHub OAuth: system browser + loopback callback, with Device Flow fallback
- Secure token storage (OS-level)
- User profile, status bar, Settings integration
- Logout, token revocation, minimal scopes

## Definition of Done
- [ ] Login opens the system browser and validates state and callback
- [ ] Device Flow fallback works without a callback port
- [ ] Tokens are stored in secure storage and never in renderer localStorage
- [ ] Status bar shows login state
- [ ] Logout removes tokens and clears the UI session

## Milestone
0.1.x Bootstrap MVP

issues.title: AD-6 Phase 6: Model Gateway and first chat vertical slice
issues.body:
## Objective
Define a provider contract for LLM providers and implement a minimal chat tab that invokes a model.

## Scope
- Provider contract: model list, health check, streaming, tool calling, context window
- Adapters: OpenRouter, Ollama, LM Studio, generic OpenAI-compatible adapter
- Chat tab: model selection, message sending, streaming, stop/cancel
- Event log: user message, model metadata, streaming chunks, final response

## Definition of Done
- [ ] Provider contract is normalized across adapters
- [ ] Chat tab supports model selection and message sending
- [ ] Streaming responses without blocking the UI
- [ ] Stop/cancel interrupts the request
- [ ] Event log records session entries without secrets

## Milestone
0.2.x Agent Runtime and MCP

issues.title: AD-7 Phase 7: Tool Router, file tool, patch model and Permission Broker
issues.body:
## Objective
Provide file tools for the first slice with a deny-first approval flow and a Conflict Broker.

## Scope
- Read-only tools: read file, search (with size/scope limits)
- Mutating tool `applyPatch` with an approval workflow
- PatchSet: base hash, operations, author, risk level
- Conflict Broker: auto-merge non-conflicting changes, review gate for conflicts
- Sensitive files: separate high-risk approval path

## Definition of Done
- [ ] Read-only tools expose explicit limits
- [ ] `applyPatch` requires approval before applying
- [ ] A patch conflicting with the current file version creates a `Conflict`
- [ ] Diff is visible in the UI and linked to the event log
- [ ] Delete/rename/binary/high-risk operations require an additional explicit decision

## Milestone
0.2.x Agent Runtime and MCP

issues.title: AD-8 Phase 8: Agent Runtime, workers and subagents
issues.body:
## Objective
Implement a Session Broker with isolated workers, subagents, and task activity tracking.

## Scope
- Session Broker: `AgentSession` per `ChatTab`, isolated context
- Worker lifecycle: start/stop, retry, cancellation, crash recovery
- Subagents: separate tasks with limited context that return a summary to the parent
- Task Activity: status, tools used, result

## Definition of Done
- [ ] Each `ChatTab` has its own session, model, agent, and event log
- [ ] Worker stops gracefully without killing the runtime
- [ ] Worker crashes are reported in the UI with an option to resume
- [ ] Subagent returns a summary rather than full history
- [ ] Permission Broker distinguishes parent and subagent scopes

## Milestone
0.2.x Agent Runtime and MCP

issues.title: AD-9 Phase 9: Memory Service and Code Indexer
issues.body:
## Objective
Provide local memory (Markdown + SQLite/sqlite-vec) and semantic code retrieval.

## Scope
- SQLite schema: event log, patches, sessions, memories, embeddings, migrations
- `sqlite-vec` for embeddings with model metadata
- File-based memory: `MemoryEntry` pointing to a Markdown file, scope, checksum
- Tree-sitter chunking: TS/JS/JSON/YAML/Markdown/PowerShell
- RetrievalQuery: filters for scope, language, folder, freshness

## Definition of Done
- [ ] Versioned SQLite schema with migrations
- [ ] Append-only event log with secret redaction
- [ ] `sqlite-vec` stores embeddings with metadata
- [ ] Agent suggests memory changes as a patch (not silent write)
- [ ] Index is rebuildable deterministically

## Milestone
0.2.x Agent Runtime and MCP

issues.title: AD-10 Phase 10: MCP Manager
issues.body:
## Objective
Manage `mcp.json`, the lifecycle of stdio/http servers, trust decisions and routing of tools/resources/prompts.

## Scope
- MCP configuration: user/workspace scope, `mcp.json`
- Lifecycle states: disabled, needs trust, starting, running, failed, stopped
- Trust decision per workspace/user
- Tools/resources/prompts routing via Tool Router and Permission Broker
- Handling timeouts, invalid responses and protocol negotiation failures

## Definition of Done
- [ ] MCP Manager detects user and workspace configurations
- [ ] Servers expose a full set of lifecycle statuses
- [ ] Starting a workspace server requires trust approval
- [ ] Each MCP tool call goes through the Permission Broker
- [ ] Integration tests with mock MCP servers (stdio + HTTP)

issues.title: AD-11 Phase 11: Extension Host, VSIX Installer and extension compatibility
issues.body:
## Objective
Support installing extensions from a folder and VSIX, provide a minimal `vscode` shim, and maintain a compatibility matrix.

## Scope
- Parse manifests: `package.json`, `engines.vscode`, contribution points
- VSIX Installer: validation, unpack, local install
- `vscode` shim: commands, configuration, views, menus, languages, diagnostics, testing, debug
- Compatibility matrix: SonarQube, GitHub Actions, Playwright Test, PowerShell, Context7 MCP, PDF Utilities MCP

## Definition of Done
- [ ] Load extension from a folder and from a `.vsix`
- [ ] Installer validates manifest, size and structure
- [ ] Invalid VSIX does not leave a partial installation behind
- [ ] Supported contribution points for the MVP are implemented
- [ ] Unsupported APIs return a controlled error

## Milestone
0.3.x Extension Host and compatibility

issues.title: AD-12 Phase 12: Terminal, Problems, Output, Testing, Debug and LSP bridge
issues.body:
## Objective
Provide minimal IDE services required by priority extensions.

## Scope
- Problems panel: diagnostics from LSP/extensions, navigation to Monaco
- Output channels: service/extension logs, filtering
- Terminal Service: Windows profiles, log redaction
- Testing API: test tree, run profiles, results
- Debug Service: minimal launch/stop (PowerShell)
- LSP bridge: stdio transport, process spawn

## Definition of Done
- [ ] Problems panel displays diagnostics and navigates to Monaco
- [ ] Output channels support source filtering
- [ ] Terminal runs basic Windows profiles
- [ ] Testing API supports test tree and run profiles
- [ ] Debug launch/stop works for PowerShell
- [ ] Smoke tests: SonarQube, GitHub Actions, Playwright Test, PowerShell

## Milestone
0.3.x Extension Host and compatibility

issues.title: AD-13 Phase 13: Settings, secrets, docs and MVP hardening
issues.body:
## Objective
Implement a Settings Service, secure secrets handling, MVP documentation and hardening.

## Scope
- Settings Service: user/workspace scope, secrets in secure storage
- UI Settings: JSON fallback, schema validation
- Documentation: architecture, security model, running instructions, compatibility matrix
- MVP limitations: no full Windows sandbox, not full VS Code API compatibility

## Definition of Done
- [ ] Settings Service separates scopes and secrets
- [ ] Secrets are stored in secure storage or in environment variables `${env:VAR_NAME}`
- [ ] UI Settings provide a JSON fallback and validation
- [ ] README describes AgentDeck as its own IDE (not an official MS distribution)
- [ ] Security documentation covers IPC, deny-first, sensitive paths, and secure storage
- [ ] MVP limitations are documented

## Milestone
1.0 Stabilize MVP

issues.title: AD-14 Phase 14: Automated validation and MVP readiness
issues.body:
## Objective
Establish quality gates, E2E tests for a vertical slice, and prepare for code review.

## Scope
- Typecheck, lint, unit tests for renderer/main/preload/services/runtime
- Integration tests: IPC, Workspace Service, Model Gateway, MCP mock, SQLite, Extension Host
- E2E tests: open `.code-workspace` -> Explorer -> Monaco -> chat -> model mock -> file tool -> patch -> diff
- A11y tests: workbench, chat, approvals, settings, MCP Manager, extension view
- Architectural tests: dependency-cruiser, no cycles, no imports from `internal`

## Definition of Done
- [ ] Typecheck, lint and unit tests pass
- [ ] Integration tests pass
- [ ] E2E vertical-slice tests pass
- [ ] A11y tests pass
- [ ] Architectural tests are enforced
- [ ] Coverage report: Permission Broker, Tool Router, Model Gateway, MCP Manager, Memory Service, Extension Host
- [ ] Limitations that cannot be automatically verified are documented

## Milestone
1.0 Stabilize MVP

issues.title: AD-16 Add standalone general CI workflow
issues.body:
## Objective
Add a standalone `ci.yml` workflow that runs AgentDeck's core validation independently from SonarCloud.

## Context
The existing `SonarCloud Analysis` workflow already runs typecheck, lint, coverage, architecture tests and build before the scan. Those commands should also run in a general CI path that does not require SonarCloud availability or `SONAR_TOKEN`.

## Scope
- Add `.github/workflows/ci.yml` for `push` and `pull_request` events on `main` and version branches
- Use Node.js 22 with npm caching and `npm ci --ignore-scripts`
- Run `npm run typecheck`, `npm run lint`, `npm run test:unit`, `npm run test:architecture` and `npm run build`
- Keep the workflow independent from SonarCloud, SonarScanner and `SONAR_TOKEN`
- Use minimal GitHub Actions permissions and a bounded timeout

## Rationale
SonarCloud analysis should continue to run quality analysis, but pull requests need a basic validation path even if the external service, scanner action or token is temporarily unavailable.

## Benefits
- Reduces the risk of PR validation being blocked by a third-party service outage
- Keeps core feedback available within a few minutes
- Makes branch protection able to require general CI separately from SonarCloud analysis

## Definition of Done
- [ ] `.github/workflows/ci.yml` exists and is separate from `.github/workflows/sonar.yml`
- [ ] CI runs on pushes and pull requests to `main` and version branches
- [ ] CI installs dependencies with `npm ci --ignore-scripts`
- [ ] CI runs typecheck, lint, unit tests, architecture tests and build
- [ ] CI does not reference SonarCloud, SonarScanner, `SONAR_TOKEN` or `sonar-project.properties`
- [ ] README or CI documentation names the general CI check if branch protection should require it
- [ ] Existing SonarCloud workflow remains focused on analysis and keeps using the generated coverage report

## Milestone
1.0 Stabilizacja MVP

issues.title: AD-17 Add Dependabot, npm audit and CodeQL as a complement to SonarCloud
issues.body:
## Objective
Add Dependabot (GitHub Dependabot alerts/PRs), an `npm audit` CI step and CodeQL scanning for JavaScript/TypeScript as a complement to SonarCloud.

## Context
Following the SonarCloud integration, we should add a dedicated dependency and security scanning layer to detect vulnerable npm packages, supply chain issues, and CodeQL security query findings. SonarCloud focuses on code quality and some SAST rules, but SCA (Software Composition Analysis) and CodeQL security queries provide additional coverage.

## Scope
- Configure Dependabot for npm (`.github/dependabot.yml`) with a weekly update schedule and sensible ecosystem settings.
- Add an `npm audit` job/step to CI that runs `npm ci --ignore-scripts` and `npm audit --audit-level=moderate --json` and fails or warns per policy.
- Add a `.github/workflows/codeql.yml` workflow configured for `javascript` and `typescript` using the default CodeQL packs.
- Document the remediation policy (reviewers, auto-merge rules, severity thresholds) in `SECURITY.md` or `docs/`.

## Rationale
SonarCloud is valuable for SAST and quality metrics, but third-party dependency vulnerabilities and CodeQL security detections are a different risk category. Combining SCA (Dependabot/npm audit) with CodeQL increases our defense-in-depth.

## Benefits
- Better detection of vulnerable npm packages and supply-chain issues.
- Faster time-to-remediation for CVEs through Dependabot PRs.
- Additional CodeQL detections for security anti-patterns not covered by SonarCloud.
- Improves overall security posture and reduces dependency-regression risk.

## Definition of Done
- [ ] `.github/dependabot.yml` exists with an appropriate schedule and scope.
- [ ] CI contains an `npm audit` job/step producing machine-readable output and enforcing the audit policy.
- [ ] `.github/workflows/codeql.yml` is configured for `javascript`/`typescript` and enabled.
- [ ] Documentation in `SECURITY.md` or `docs/` describes the dependency remediation policy and CodeQL review process.
- [ ] Labels/triage rules defined for Dependabot PRs (e.g., `dependabot`, `security`, `priority:high`).

## Milestone
1.0 Stabilizacja MVP

issues.title: AD-18 Expand vscode shim to broader VS Code API compatibility
issues.body:
## Objective
Expand the `vscode` shim to cover additional namespaces and APIs to increase extension compatibility.

## Context
The current `vscode` shim implements a minimal subset of the VS Code Extension APIs required by priority extensions. A larger compatibility surface will enable more extensions to run with fewer runtime errors and lower migration effort.

## Scope
- Extend the `vscode` shim with additional namespaces (for example: `scm`, `comment`, `notebook`, `webview`, `customEditors`) and common API surfaces used by web extensions and notebooks.
- Implement compatibility behaviors for web extensions and common `vscode.env` / `vscode.workspace` interactions.
- Provide graceful fallback shims and feature-detection that log clear warnings when an API is not supported.
- Add integration tests and sample extensions exercising SCM, Comments API, Notebooks and Custom Editors.
- Document limitations and provide a migration guide for extension authors.

## Rationale
Full VS Code API parity is a large product; the MVP implemented only the minimal subset. Targeted expansion will allow more extensions to work in AgentDeck and reduce engineering friction for users migrating from VS Code.

## Benefits
- Increased number of working extensions and reduced migration effort for users.
- Fewer runtime errors for installed extensions and clearer guidance for unsupported features.
- Better compatibility testing for priority extension scenarios (notebooks, SCM, comments, custom editors).

## Definition of Done
- [ ] `vscode` shim implements the listed namespaces with documented limitations.
- [ ] Integration tests or sample extensions exercise SCM, Comments API, Notebooks and Custom Editors.
- [ ] Migration guide and compatibility notes added to `docs/compatibility.md` or similar.
- [ ] Labels and roadmap entry created.

## Milestone
1.0 Stabilizacja MVP

issues.title: AD-19 Cloud embedding uplift
issues.body:
## Objective
Add optional premium cloud embeddings (for example `text-embedding-3-large`) alongside the local/default `bge-m3`.

## Context
The MVP is designed to operate locally with predictable costs, but some teams may accept cloud costs for improved retrieval quality. Offering an optional premium path allows teams to choose quality over cost where appropriate.

## Scope
- Add configuration to select embeddings provider: local `bge-m3` (default) or optional premium cloud models (example: `text-embedding-3-large`).
- Implement a provider interface and selection logic with safe fallback to local embeddings when cloud is unavailable.
- Add telemetry and benchmark scripts to compare retrieval quality and cost across representative datasets.
- Implement quota/cost limits, rate-limiting and explicit opt-in confirmation for premium embeddings.
- Document usage and cost implications in `docs/embedding.md`.

## Rationale
Local, predictable costs support the MVP, but offering premium cloud embeddings can materially improve semantic search quality in very large repositories where local embeddings are insufficient.

## Benefits
- Improved semantic search relevance for large codebases and monorepos.
- Fewer false-positive context reads and better retrieval precision.
- Flexibility for teams to trade cost for retrieval quality.

## Definition of Done
- [ ] Configuration option exists to select embeddings provider.
- [ ] Example implementation for a premium provider (`text-embedding-3-large`) with safe fallback to `bge-m3`.
- [ ] Benchmark scripts and documentation in `docs/embedding.md`.
- [ ] Labels and roadmap entry created.

## Milestone
1.0 Stabilizacja MVP

issues.title: AD-20 Automated guard for pinning third-party actions
issues.body:
## Objective
Add an automated workflow or policy that detects `uses:` entries without a pinned full SHA in GitHub Actions workflows.

## Context
AD-17 recommends pinning new Actions, but the repository does not yet enforce this rule across existing workflows. A separate automation can scan workflows and flag `uses:` lines that omit a full 40-character SHA.

## Scope
- Implement a scanner workflow or policy that inspects `.github/workflows/**` for `uses:` entries without a full SHA.
- Surface findings as a CI check, PR comment, or an automated issue, and optionally fail CI on new unpinned `uses:` entries.
- Optionally provide an automated suggestion/PR that replaces loose refs with the latest pinned SHA for maintainers to review.
- Document the policy and remediation steps in `SECURITY.md` or `docs/workflow-security.md`.

## Rationale
Manual review of workflow pins is error prone. Automated detection reduces supply-chain risk by ensuring PRs introducing unpinned third-party Actions are flagged early.

## Benefits
- Reduces supply-chain regression risk from unpinned Actions.
- Lowers review burden by automatically flagging problematic workflow changes.
- Provides clear remediation guidance or automated suggestions.

## Definition of Done
- [ ] A scanner workflow or policy exists that detects unpinned `uses:` in `.github/workflows/**`.
- [ ] The scanner reports results as a CI check or PR comment on offending changes.
- [ ] Documentation describes the enforcement policy and remediation steps.

## Milestone
1.0 Stabilizacja MVP

issues.title: AD-21 Update electron-vite to stable v6 after GA release
issues.body:
## Objective
Update the `electron-vite` pin in `package.json` from `6.0.0-beta.1` to the stable `6.0.0` once it is released without a `-beta` suffix.

## Context
The current plan intentionally uses `electron-vite@6.0.0-beta.1` because it is the only available release supporting Vite 8. A stable GA release will eliminate the risk of further breaking changes before official release.

## Scope
- Monitor the `electron-vite` npm registry for a stable `6.0.0` (or later) release without a prerelease suffix.
- Update the version pin in `package.json` and run `npm install`.
- Run the full validation suite (typecheck, lint, unit tests, architecture tests, build, E2E) to confirm no regressions.
- Update `CHANGELOG.md` and any documentation referencing the beta version.
- Remove any workarounds or beta-specific configuration introduced for `6.0.0-beta.1`.

## Rationale
Using a beta dependency in production increases the risk of unexpected breaking changes on every install. A stable release provides a fixed, tested API surface and removes the need for the `@beta` npm tag.

## Benefits
- Eliminates the risk of unexpected breaking changes on fresh `npm install`.
- Removes the need for the `@beta` npm dist-tag during installation.
- Greater confidence in the stability of the `electron-vite` public API.
- Cleaner dependency manifest without prerelease markers.

## Definition of Done
- [ ] `package.json` pins `electron-vite` at a stable version (no `-beta` suffix).
- [ ] Full validation suite passes after the update.
- [ ] `CHANGELOG.md` documents the version bump.
- [ ] Beta-specific workarounds are removed.

## Milestone
1.0 Stabilizacja MVP

issues.title: AD-22 Investigate migration to Vite 8 oxc config over esbuild compat
issues.body:
## Objective
Investigate and plan the migration from the deprecated `esbuild` config field to the native Vite 8 `oxc` configuration in `electron.vite.config.ts`.

## Context
Vite 8 introduces a native `oxc` configuration namespace as the replacement for the `esbuild` field, which is deprecated. The project currently does not use any `esbuild` options, so there is no immediate breakage, but future configuration needs (for example `define`, `jsxInject`) should use the `oxc.*` path.

## Scope
- Audit the current `electron.vite.config.ts` for any `esbuild` field usage.
- Research the Vite 8 `oxc` configuration API and map any future `esbuild` options to their `oxc` equivalents.
- Document the migration path and recommended configuration patterns in `docs/vite-config.md` or similar.
- If the project adds JS transformation options (define, jsxInject, targets, etc.), implement them under `oxc.*` rather than `esbuild.*`.
- Add a deprecation warning or lint rule that flags new `esbuild.*` usage in the config.

## Rationale
The project does not currently use `esbuild` options, so there is no urgency. However, preparing the migration path now avoids a breaking-change surprise when Vite removes the `esbuild` compat layer in a future major release.

## Benefits
- Proactively prepares the configuration for the removal of the `esbuild` compat layer.
- Reduces the risk of a breaking change on the next Vite major update.
- Provides clear guidance for contributors adding transformation options in the future.

## Definition of Done
- [ ] Audit confirms no `esbuild` field usage in `electron.vite.config.ts`.
- [ ] Migration path and `oxc` equivalents documented.
- [ ] Any new transformation options use `oxc.*` instead of `esbuild.*`.
- [ ] Lint rule or deprecation warning flags new `esbuild.*` usage.

## Milestone
1.0 Stabilizacja MVP
